Al Jazeera — Pentagon readies for weeks of US ground operations in Iran

title: Spain bans US military flights through its airspace — first NATO ally to formally defy US over Iran war
description: Spanish Defense Minister Margarita Robles announced Spain is closing its airspace to US military aircraft involved in the Iran war, citing Spain's long-standing opposition to the US-Israel military campaign. Spain joins a growing number of European governments expressing alarm over the conflict's escalation. This marks the first formal act of defiance by a NATO member state against US military operations, creating a significant fracture in the Western alliance. Trump responded by threatening trade tariffs against Spain, raising the prospect of a simultaneous trade war with a NATO ally.
impact: First NATO ally to formally restrict US military access marks a watershed in alliance cohesion. Trump's trade war threats compound the diplomatic crisis. If other NATO members follow Spain's lead, it could materially limit US air corridors for the Iran campaign and trigger a broader European-American split.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Spain closes airspace to US military flights over Iran war

title: IDF strikes 120+ targets in Tehran overnight — largest single-night bombing wave of the war
description: The IDF conducted its most intense single-night air campaign of the war, striking over 120 targets in and around Tehran including missile production facilities, weapons storage sites, and IRGC command infrastructure. The wave occurred overnight March 29-30. Iranian state media confirmed 'widespread explosions' across multiple Tehran districts. Mehrabad Airport was struck for the second time. Iran's civil aviation authority suspended all domestic and international flights out of Tehran through the morning.
impact: 120+ targets in a single night marks the most intensive bombing run of the 31-day conflict. Mehrabad Airport re-struck. Tehran airport operations halted. Iran's missile production infrastructure, already assessed at 70% degraded, subjected to further degradation. Civilian population in major urban center subjected to largest overnight bombing campaign since the war began.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran-Israel war live updates March 30

title: Tabriz Petrochemical Company struck — IDF escalates targeting of Iran's civilian industrial base
description: Israeli aircraft struck the Tabriz Petrochemical Company in northwestern Iran, one of Iran's largest petrochemical production facilities. The strike caused a major fire visible from the city center. Tabriz is Iran's fourth-largest city and a major industrial hub. The strike represents a significant escalation in targeting civilian industrial infrastructure beyond strictly military and energy targets, drawing condemnation from international observers. Several workers were reported injured in the attack.
impact: Petrochemical infrastructure strike signals IDF broadening target set to Iran's civilian industrial base. Tabriz strike follows Bandar Khamir port strike (Day 30). Iran's petrochemical sector — its second-largest export earner after crude oil — now under direct attack. Potential war crimes concerns raised by targeting civilian industrial facilities.

title: Iranian drone kills Indian worker at Kuwait power plant — war reaches third-country civilian infrastructure
description: An Iranian drone struck a power and desalination plant in Kuwait, killing one Indian worker and injuring several others. This marks the first confirmed third-country civilian casualty from an Iranian strike on Gulf civilian infrastructure during the war. Kuwait is a US ally hosting American forces but has not participated in combat operations. The strike on a power/desalination plant — civilian critical infrastructure — prompted emergency diplomatic protests from Kuwait, India, and the UN. Iran did not claim responsibility.
impact: War spreads to third-country civilian infrastructure for the first time. Kuwait civilian casualty escalates regional tensions. India — with 700,000 citizens in Kuwait alone — faces pressure to respond diplomatically. Kuwait's ability to remain neutral while hosting US forces comes under new strain. Desalination plant damage threatens water supply in a water-scarce nation.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iranian drone strike kills Indian worker in Kuwait

title: Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery struck — Iran/Hezbollah escalate to Israeli energy infrastructure
description: A barrage of Iranian ballistic missiles and Hezbollah rockets struck the Bazan Oil Refinery in Haifa, Israel's largest refinery complex and primary fuel supply hub for the north. A major fire broke out at one of the refinery's processing units, requiring emergency response. One person was lightly wounded near the refinery perimeter. The Bazan refinery processes approximately 9.8 million tonnes of crude per year — roughly 25% of Israel's total refining capacity. The strike represents the first confirmed direct hit on a major Israeli energy processing facility.
impact: Haifa Bazan strike hits Israel's largest oil refinery, threatening domestic fuel supply and potentially triggering fuel rationing in northern Israel. Refinery fire may take weeks to extinguish. Israel's energy infrastructure now a confirmed Iranian target. Symmetric energy infrastructure targeting escalation: Israel targets Tabriz Petrochemical, Iran targets Haifa Bazan on same day.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Haifa refinery fire following missile barrage

title: Iran advancing NPT withdrawal legislation — would exit nuclear non-proliferation framework
description: Iranian parliamentarians introduced priority legislation to withdraw Iran from the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT), citing US and Israeli strikes on nuclear facilities as justification. Parliament's national security commission spokesperson Ebrahim Rezaei: 'The NPT has offered us no advantage whatsoever.' Tehran MP Malek Shariati uploaded formal legislation to parliamentary portal. Iran's stated rationale: NPT provided no protection and allowed 'spying by IAEA inspectors.' Iran stressed this is not a move toward nuclear weapons but a rejection of the NPT framework under wartime conditions.
impact: Iran exiting the NPT would remove IAEA inspection rights, eliminate legal constraints on weapons development, and signal possible nuclear weapons pursuit to the international community. Would likely trigger new UN Security Council emergency session and escalatory responses from US and Israel.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Lawmakers push NPT exit as US-Israel hit Iran's nuclear sites
Tasnim News — Iran begins considering withdrawal from NPT

title: 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two March 30 incidents — France calls emergency UN Security Council session
description: Two separate incidents on March 30 killed three UNIFIL peacekeepers and wounded five more — the deadliest single day for UN forces in the conflict. In the first incident, an Indonesian peacekeeper was killed and one critically injured at a UN position near Adchit Al-Qusayr. In the second, two peacekeepers were killed when an explosion destroyed their vehicle near Bani Hayyan; a third was severely injured. France immediately called an emergency UN Security Council meeting. UN Secretary-General Guterres stated the attacks 'may amount to war crimes.' UNIFIL investigations are ongoing.
impact: Deadliest single day for UNIFIL peacekeepers in this conflict. Three killed in one day — more than all previous combined. France's emergency UN Security Council call raises pressure for international intervention. Attacks on peacekeepers, if attributed to a party, could trigger NATO Article 5 or broader international response. Indonesia — largest UNIFIL contributor (~1,200 troops) — faces domestic political pressure to withdraw or escalate. Guterres war crimes language is the strongest yet from a UN official in this conflict.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — UN peacekeeper killed as Israel-Hezbollah fighting escalates in Lebanon
Alma Research Center — Second Iran War March 30 daily report

title: Iran declares private residences of US and Israeli officials 'legitimate targets' — major targeting doctrine shift
description: Iran's joint military command spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari declared that the private residences of US and Israeli officials are now 'legitimate targets' for Iranian retaliation. This marks a significant and unprecedented escalation in declared targeting policy — extending the declared target set from military and government facilities to the personal homes of officials and their families. The statement comes as Trump threatened to 'obliterate' Iran's civilian infrastructure (power plants, oil wells, desalination plants), suggesting a potential tit-for-tat targeting escalation.
impact: Unprecedented declared targeting of civilian-adjacent personal property. Creates new psychological threat dimension for US and Israeli government personnel. If acted upon, would constitute a war crime under international humanitarian law. Mirrors and escalates Trump's threat to obliterate Iranian civilian infrastructure. May be a deterrence tactic but signals willingness to cross the civilian targeting threshold.

title: IRGC Commander Hassanzadeh eliminated in AI-targeted strike — senior Tehran security commander killed
description: Israeli strike killed Hassan Hassanzadeh, Commander of the IRGC Rasul-Allah Corps (Tehran's security unit), reportedly using AI-powered intelligence targeting platforms. Hassanzadeh is among the highest-ranking IRGC commanders eliminated since the war began. His death further degrades Iran's command and control structure in the capital.
impact: Further decapitation of IRGC leadership in Tehran. AI-targeting component signals advanced intelligence penetration. Cumulative senior officer losses (~40+ officials killed) severely impacting Iran's military decision-making chain.

sources.name: Alma Research Center — Daily Report March 30

title: Iranian drone strikes fully laden Kuwaiti oil tanker at Dubai port — first direct hit on commercial vessel in port
description: IRGC drone struck the Al-Salmi, a fully laden Kuwaiti VLCC, at anchor in Dubai's port area. Fire broke out on the hull-damaged vessel. This is the first Iranian strike on a commercial vessel actually in port (vs. at sea or in transit), significantly escalating the naval dimension of the conflict. Oil spill risk from the fully loaded tanker threatens Dubai's port operations and coastal environment.
impact: First strike on a commercial vessel in port rather than at sea. Threatens all Gulf port operations. Oil spill risk. Insurance premiums for Gulf ports will surge. Could trigger UAE military response. Further demonstrates Iran's ability to project force throughout the Gulf.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Iran Strikes Kuwait Oil Tanker in Dubai Port
Al Jazeera liveblog — Kuwaiti tanker hit in Dubai port

title: NATO intercepts 4th Iranian ballistic missile in Turkish airspace — Article 5 spillover risk intensifies
description: NATO air defense systems intercepted a fourth Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace. Turkey confirmed the interception while Iran's embassy denied responsibility. Fourth such incident raises serious questions about whether Iran is deliberately or accidentally firing into NATO territory.
impact: Four missiles into NATO territory creates mounting pressure for Article 5 invocation. Turkey — a NATO member that has maintained diplomatic ties with Iran — faces growing domestic pressure to respond. If attributed as deliberate, could trigger NATO collective defense response.

sources.name: Bloomberg — NATO Intercepts Fourth Iranian Missile Targeting Turkey
Al-Monitor — Turkey says NATO defenses intercepted fourth missile

title: Israeli airstrike hits permanent Lebanese Army checkpoint — first direct strike on LAF installation
description: Israeli airstrike struck a fixed Lebanese Armed Forces checkpoint at al-Amiriyah near Qleileh, killing soldier Ali Ajam and wounding 5 including one officer critically. This is the first direct Israeli strike on a permanent LAF military installation, crossing a threshold that previously distinguished the LAF (a non-combatant) from Hezbollah.
impact: Blurs the line between Hezbollah targets and Lebanese state military infrastructure. Could push Lebanon's army into active opposition to Israel. May complicate post-war stabilization if LAF is no longer viewed as a neutral force.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Lebanese soldier killed in Israeli strike

title: IMF: 'All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth' — largest oil market disruption in history
description: The IMF issued a major March 30 warning that the Iran war constitutes the largest disruption to the global oil market in history. IMF economists wrote that 'all roads lead to higher prices and slower growth.' OECD raised US inflation forecast to 4.2% (+1.2pp). Goldman Sachs puts US recession probability at 30%. Low-income countries face acute food insecurity from fertilizer shortages.
impact: Official IMF characterization as 'largest oil disruption in history' signals unprecedented global economic stress. 30% recession probability in the world's largest economy. Developing world food crisis looming from Hormuz fertilizer blockade. IMF full assessment due April 14.

sources.name: Bloomberg — IMF Says Iran War Means Higher Prices, Slower Growth
Al-Monitor — Iran war dimming outlook for many economies

title: 4 IDF soldiers killed in Hezbollah ambush in southern Lebanon — deadliest single engagement
description: Nahal Brigade Reconnaissance Unit ambushed Hezbollah cell in western southern Lebanon. Close-range firefight, anti-tank missile fired during evacuation. 4 killed (Cpt. Madmoni, SSgt. Cohen, SSgt. Entis + 1), 2 wounded. Deadliest single IDF engagement in the Lebanon front since the war began.
impact: Deadliest single IDF engagement in Lebanon front. Raises domestic Israeli pressure. Demonstrates Hezbollah's continued capability despite Israeli operations.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Four soldiers killed in south Lebanon

title: Missile strikes Haifa Bazan oil refinery — gasoline tank fire, 2nd hit during war
description: Iran/Hezbollah missile struck gasoline storage tank at Bazan Group refinery in Haifa. Fire contained. 2 injured (1 serious). Second strike on the facility (first March 19). Energy Minister said production unaffected.
impact: Second successful hit on Israel's primary oil refinery. Demonstrates air defense gaps for energy infrastructure.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Fire after missile attack on Haifa refinery

title: WSJ: Trump willing to end war without reopening Hormuz — major strategic shift
description: WSJ reports Trump told aides he's willing to end the campaign even if Hormuz remains closed. Would leave reopening for later. Assessed reopening operations would push conflict beyond 4-6 week timeline. Abandons one of the war's stated objectives.
impact: Abandoning Hormuz reopening leaves global oil markets disrupted indefinitely. Allies dependent on Hormuz transit face sustained energy crisis. Undermines war justification for energy security.

sources.name: Haaretz — Trump tells aides willing to end without Hormuz

title: FT: Hegseth broker attempted defense ETF investment before war — insider trading allegations
description: Financial Times reports Hegseth's Morgan Stanley broker attempted multimillion-dollar investment in BlackRock iShares Defense Industrials ETF before the war. Investment didn't execute. Pentagon calls report 'entirely false and fabricated.'
impact: If substantiated, would constitute insider trading by the Defense Secretary. Could trigger Congressional investigation. Pentagon's strong denial suggests contested story.

title: Iranian cluster munition warhead strikes central Israel — 12 wounded including 6 children
description: Iran fired missile with cluster munition warhead that penetrated defenses over Bnei Brak, Ramat Gan, and Petah Tikva, dispersing ~24 bomblets. 12 wounded including 6 children. HRW condemns as unlawful. First confirmed cluster munition use by Iran against civilian areas.
impact: Cluster munitions are banned by 123 nations under Convention on Cluster Munitions. Use against civilian areas escalates international condemnation. 6 children wounded raises humanitarian crisis profile.

title: Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC at Dubai port — first tanker attack in UAE waters
description: IRGC drone struck the Kuwaiti VLCC Al-Salmi (332m, 2M barrels crude) at Dubai port anchorage. Fire contained, no spill, 24 crew safe. Expands Iran's maritime targeting to UAE waters — previously limited to Hormuz approaches. 25th commercial vessel attacked since closure.
impact: Iran attacking ships in UAE territorial waters marks significant escalation beyond Hormuz. Threatens all Gulf port operations. Could trigger insurance withdrawal from entire Gulf coast, not just strait.

title: 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed — first UN fatalities; France seeks emergency UNSC meeting
description: Three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two incidents in south Lebanon within 24 hours. First UNIFIL fatalities in this conflict. France seeks emergency UN Security Council meeting. IDF says investigating.
impact: UNIFIL deaths could trigger international demand for ceasefire. Indonesia may withdraw contingent. France leveraging for UNSC action. Sets precedent for potential escalation against peacekeeping forces.

title: Iran Parliament formalizes Hormuz toll plan — bans US/Israeli ships, claims sovereignty
description: Iranian parliament approved Hormuz toll plan with rial-denominated fees, bans on US/Israeli vessels and ships from sanctioning countries. Ghalibaf warned of US plan to seize Iranian island. Formalizes blockade as Iranian law, making any negotiated reopening harder.
impact: Parliamentary codification makes Hormuz closure an Iranian legal position rather than military action. Any deal to reopen now requires legislative reversal. Revenue structure ($600-800M/month) creates economic incentive to maintain blockade.

title: Israeli airstrike kills Lebanese Army soldier at checkpoint — first LAF installation struck
description: Israeli airstrike hit al-Amiriyah checkpoint near Qleileh. LAF soldier Ali Ajam killed, 5 wounded. First direct Israeli strike on a permanent Lebanese Armed Forces installation in this round of hostilities.
impact: Crossing the LAF red line risks pushing Lebanese military from neutral/defensive posture toward active opposition. Could fracture fragile US-Lebanon military relationship.

title: B-52 bombers fly overland missions over Iran for first time
description: Pentagon announced B-52 bombers are conducting overland missions over Iran for the first time. Previously only stealth B-2 and B-1 bombers were used. Signifies US confidence that Iran's air defenses have been fully destroyed.
impact: B-52s are non-stealth, slow, and highly vulnerable — their deployment signals total US air superiority over Iran. Psychological and strategic escalation as Iran cannot defend its airspace.

sources.name: Times of Israel — B-52 overland missions

title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad by Kataib Hezbollah
description: American journalist Shelly Kittleson (Al-Monitor) kidnapped by armed men near Palestine Hotel in Baghdad. State Department confirmed suspect has ties to Kataib Hezbollah. Iraqi forces arrested one kidnapper. Kittleson not recovered.
impact: First kidnapping of US journalist in the conflict. Escalates Iraq proxy dimension. Could trigger US retaliation against Kataib Hezbollah assets.

title: China-Pakistan unveil first formal ceasefire plan from major power
description: China and Pakistan issued 5-point plan: immediate ceasefire, peace talks, end non-military strikes, Hormuz restoration, UN framework. First formal peace plan from a major power. Marks China's transition from neutral observer to active mediator.
impact: Shifts diplomatic landscape by introducing alternative peace framework outside US control. Could divide international opinion on ceasefire terms. Trump did not criticize the plan.

title: UNSC emergency session on UNIFIL peacekeepers — first UN fatalities in conflict
description: UNSC convened emergency session after 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in Lebanon within 24 hours. France and Indonesia requested emergency meeting. Guterres called deaths 'grave violations.' Responsible party under investigation.
impact: UNIFIL deaths could trigger peacekeeping withdrawal, removing buffer between Israel and Lebanon. International pressure for ceasefire escalates.

title: Brent crude best monthly gain since inception (1988) — May $118.35 settle
description: Brent crude May contract settled at $118.35 on expiry — best monthly gain since Brent inception in 1988 (60%+ in March). WTI gained ~51% in March (best since May 2020). Gas crossed $4.018 nationally. S&P 500 had worst March since 2022.
impact: Record oil price surge threatens global recession. IMF warns of 'higher prices, slower growth.' $4 gas threshold politically significant for US public opinion on war.

title: IDF strikes chemical factory — claims Iran weaponizing fentanyl
description: IDF struck Tofiq Daru Company facility, accusing Iran's SPND of developing chemical weapons using fentanyl. First CW-related claim of the conflict. Iran has denied chemical weapons programs. If confirmed, represents new escalation category.
impact: Chemical weapons claim could justify expanded operations and complicate diplomatic efforts. Potential CWC violation implications. International reaction pending.

sources.name: Israel National News — IDF strikes chemical facility
Times of Israel — Fentanyl weaponization claim

title: Trump to address nation Wednesday — first prime-time address on conflict
description: White House announced Trump will deliver prime-time address at 9 PM ET Wednesday (April 2) with 'important update on Iran.' Follows Trump saying US could leave in '2-3 weeks' and that Iran 'doesn't need to agree to a deal.' Widely expected to signal wind-down.
impact: First major televised address could signal war end or escalation. Markets rallied on peace speculation. Could reshape public opinion and congressional dynamics.

title: IDF dual airstrike kills Hezbollah commander in Beirut — 7 dead
description: IDF carried out two airstrikes in Beirut targeting senior Hezbollah commander and another operative. Strikes hit Khalde highway and Jnah area. Lebanese Health Ministry: 7 killed, 24 wounded. Hezbollah confirmed commander killed.
impact: Continued decapitation campaign against Hezbollah leadership. Could trigger retaliatory rocket barrages on northern Israel.

title: Allied tensions: France, Italy, Spain deny US/Israel overflight and basing
description: France refused Israeli resupply overflight, Italy denied Sigonella base access, Spain closed airspace to US planes. Rubio warned of NATO reassessment post-war. Deepening rift between US and European allies over war conduct.
impact: NATO cohesion at risk. US may reassess alliance post-war. Logistical challenges for US/Israeli operations in theater.

title: IRGC fires 3 ballistic missile waves at Israel in one hour — 16+ wounded, child critical
description: Iranian Revolutionary Guards launched three waves of ballistic missiles at Israel within one hour. Cluster munition warheads struck Bnei Brak. 16+ wounded including 11-year-old girl in critical condition. Fourth wave intercepted. Despite claims of 75% launcher destruction, Iran retains significant strike capability.
impact: Iran demonstrating continued offensive capability despite month-long degradation campaign. Cluster munitions causing civilian casualties. Child in critical condition escalates humanitarian concerns.

sources.name: CGTN — Iran fires missiles at Israel

title: Iranian cruise missile hits QatarEnergy tanker; Kuwait airport fuel depots hit by drone
description: Three cruise missiles fired from Iran at Qatar — 2 intercepted, 1 hit QatarEnergy tanker Aqua 1 (17nm from Ras Laffan). 21 crew evacuated. Iranian drone hit KAFCO fuel tanks at Kuwait International Airport, causing massive fire. 1 killed in Fujairah from drone debris. Bahrain fire. Gulf under sustained bombardment.
impact: Iran expanding attacks to Qatar — first direct strike on Qatari energy infrastructure. Kuwait airport remains closed. Gulf states increasingly targeted. Energy infrastructure under sustained assault.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Iranian missile hit oil tanker in Qatari waters
Kuwait Times — Drones hit Kuwait airport fuel depots

title: Araghchi: Iran prepared for 6 months of war, Russia helping 'in many directions'
description: Iran FM Araghchi confirmed in Al Jazeera interview that Iran is prepared for 'at least six months' of war and 'completely ready' for a ground attack. Confirmed Russian assistance across multiple domains. Demands Lebanon included in ceasefire, reparations, sovereignty guarantees. Directly contradicts US claims of imminent deal.
impact: Iran signaling long-term resilience despite degradation. Russian military assistance confirmed at senior level. Gap between US exit rhetoric and Iranian defiance widens.

sources.name: NBC News — Iran FM rejects ceasefire

title: Trump considers leaving NATO; delivers primetime address on Iran war
description: Trump told Reuters he is 'absolutely' considering pulling US out of NATO over allies' refusal to join the Iran war. Called NATO a 'paper tiger.' Will deliver first major primetime address at 9 PM ET. Told allied countries to 'get your own oil' and 'start learning how to fight for yourself.' Potential NATO withdrawal would fundamentally reshape the global security order.
impact: NATO withdrawal threat escalates the conflict from regional to potentially global. Allied nations already reluctant to participate in Iran operations. European defense planning would need fundamental restructuring.

sources.name: NPR — Trump to address nation on Iran war
NBC News — Trump considering pulling out of NATO

title: Iran internal power split: Pezeshkian ceasefire vs Khamenei resistance
description: Iran President Pezeshkian signaled openness to ceasefire in call with European Council President Costa: 'Iran has the necessary will to end this war.' But Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei contradicted on Telegram: 'Hormuz leverage must continue. The demand of the masses is continuation of effective defense.' IRGC made no mention of ending the war. Visible power struggle between elected president and supreme leader.
impact: Iran's internal division could create opening for diplomacy — or lead to hardline faction consolidating power. Pezeshkian lacks authority over IRGC and military, which answer to supreme leader. Key question: can Pezeshkian deliver a ceasefire even if he wants one?

sources.name: CNBC — Trump says Iran asked for ceasefire
France 24 — Mojtaba Khamenei vows continued resistance

title: Kharazi struck in Tehran — senior Iran diplomat targeted, wife killed
description: Former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi, senior adviser to the Supreme Leader and key diplomatic back-channel figure, was seriously injured in an Israeli airstrike in Tehran. His wife was killed. He was reportedly coordinating Vance-Iran meeting via Pakistan. Strike seen as targeting Iran's diplomatic infrastructure.
impact: Targeting a senior diplomat overseeing peace negotiations could derail back-channel diplomacy. Signals Israel prioritizing military objectives over diplomatic off-ramps. Pakistan FM Dar was facilitating Kharazi's contacts with US side.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Former Iran FM seriously injured, wife killed

title: Coordinated triple-front Iran-Hezbollah-Houthi Passover barrage
description: First confirmed three-front coordinated attack: Iran launched 10 ballistic missiles with cluster warheads at central Israel (16 wounded including 11-year-old girl), Hezbollah fired at coastal corridor (Herzliya-Ashkelon), and Houthis launched 3rd barrage at southern Israel. Timed for Passover eve. Israel's largest single-day incoming since the war's opening salvo.
impact: Demonstrates Iran's ability to coordinate multi-front attacks despite 33 days of strikes on its military infrastructure. Cluster munitions against civilian areas draw international condemnation. 11-year-old girl fighting for life.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Iran fires largest missile barrage in weeks
Al Jazeera — Houthis claim joint missile attack

title: Trump sets April 6 Hormuz deadline — threatens energy infrastructure destruction
description: In prime-time address, Trump set April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Threatened destruction of energy plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island. Markets spiked — Brent jumped 5% to $106. Largest escalation threat since Day 1.
impact: April 6 deadline creates hard inflection point. If Iran doesn't comply, destruction of energy infrastructure would remove Iran's main revenue source and likely cause oil prices to spike to $150+. Markets treating this as highest-risk event of the war.

title: IDF eliminates 3 high-value targets in 24 hours — IRGC, Hezbollah, Basij commanders
description: Israel killed IRGC Quds Force engineering chief Vafaei (Mahallat), Hezbollah Southern Front Commander 'Hajj Sadiq' (naval strike), and confirmed Basij deputy commander Karishi (killed ~2 weeks prior). Decapitation campaign continuing despite Iran's ability to coordinate multi-front attacks.
impact: Three senior military commanders eliminated in 24 hours demonstrates Israel's intelligence penetration of Iranian/Hezbollah command structures. However, Iran's ability to still coordinate Passover barrage shows resilience of decentralized command.

title: Isfahan bunker-buster strikes cause largest secondary explosions of the war
description: Heavy bunker-buster bombs dropped on mountainous area near Isfahan causing massive secondary explosions — the largest recorded since the war began. Isfahan steel plant struck for second time that week. Al-Ahwaz steel plant in Ahvaz also hit. Over 400 strikes in 48 hours with 650+ munitions.
impact: Massive secondary explosions suggest destruction of ammunition or weapons stockpile deep underground. Scale of 400+ strikes in 48 hours with 650+ munitions shows intensification of campaign despite peace signals.

title: IRGC threatens 18 US tech companies — Apple, Google, Meta, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing
description: IRGC issued unprecedented threat listing 18 major US technology and defense companies as military targets, ordering employee evacuations. Companies include Apple, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing. First time a state actor has directly threatened named corporations as military targets.
impact: Unprecedented corporate targeting could trigger massive evacuations from Middle East, accelerate tech supply chain restructuring, and justify US escalation. Markets fell on the news. If carried out, would represent new dimension of asymmetric warfare.

title: Oil surges 8% to $109 — Trump vows to hit Iran 'extremely hard' for 2-3 more weeks
description: Brent crude surged 8% to $109.16/bbl and WTI jumped 8.2% to $108.36/bbl after Trump's primetime address vowing to hit Iran 'extremely hard' for 2-3 more weeks. Complete reversal of April 1 dip to ~$101 on peace hopes. Asia-Pacific markets plunged.
impact: 8% single-session oil spike erases all peace-hope gains. Markets now pricing in prolonged conflict. April 6 Hormuz deadline creates binary risk event. Goldman sees $110 average through April.

title: China-Pakistan 5-point peace plan — first major power ceasefire framework
description: China and Pakistan announced joint 5-point peace plan after FM Dar-Wang Yi Beijing meeting: immediate ceasefire, no force during talks, stop infrastructure attacks, restore Hormuz shipping, comprehensive peace under UN Charter. First credible major power initiative to end the war.
impact: First serious ceasefire framework from a major power. Pakistan's role as US-Iran mediator gives it credibility. Axios reports US may not oppose behind scenes. Iran's response will be critical. Links ceasefire to Hormuz reopening.

title: Pasteur Institute of Iran destroyed — healthcare infrastructure targeting escalates
description: US-Israeli airstrike destroyed the Pasteur Institute of Iran, a century-old medical research center (founded 1920) in Tehran. Iran's Health Ministry: 'barbaric assault on international health security.' 316 healthcare centers damaged since Feb 28. WHO documented 24+ healthcare attacks.
impact: Destruction of a major medical research institution crosses humanitarian thresholds. Could trigger ICC investigation. International condemnation expected.

title: US Embassy Baghdad: imminent militia attacks within 24-48 hours
description: US Embassy in Baghdad issued urgent security alert warning Iranian-backed militia groups may attack central Baghdad within 24-48 hours. US citizens told to leave immediately. Airspace closed. Targets: diplomatic facilities, energy infrastructure, hotels, airports.
impact: Strongest Baghdad warning since war began. Signals major Iraqi militia escalation. US military posture in Iraq at highest level since 2003.

title: 40-nation Hormuz coalition formed — Iran-Oman draft navigation regime
description: UK-led 40-nation conference concluded demanding Iran reopen Strait of Hormuz (US absent). Iran-Oman nearly finalized draft protocol for new navigation regime — first diplomatic framework for post-war transit.
impact: Largest multilateral Hormuz effort to date. Iran-Oman draft signals Tehran preparing post-war framework. Could create path to partial reopening before April 6 deadline.

title: Hegseth fires Army Chief Gen. Randy George during active wartime operations
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George effective immediately, replacing him with his own military aide Gen. Christopher LaNeve. First firing of an Army Chief during active combat operations in modern US history. Bipartisan congressional criticism.
impact: Major military leadership upheaval during active operations across three fronts (Iran, Lebanon, Iraq). Raises questions about civilian-military relations and command continuity.

sources.name: Breaking Defense — Hegseth fires Army chief
Washington Post — Hegseth ousts Gen. George

title: Iran cluster munitions hit playground in Petah Tikva — 11-year-old girl critical
description: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster warhead struck industrial zone in Petah Tikva. Bomblets spread to nearby playground. An 11-year-old girl critically wounded. Third documented cluster munition attack on populated civilian areas this week. HRW condemns as 'inherently indiscriminate.'
impact: Continued Iranian use of cluster munitions against populated civilian areas. Child casualties strengthen international pressure. Could trigger additional ICC referrals.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Girl critically hurt by cluster bomb

title: GCC backs UN Security Council resolution authorizing force to reopen Hormuz — vote Friday
description: Gulf Cooperation Council nations backed a draft UNSC resolution authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Bahrain (current UNSC president) proposed the resolution. Russia and China expected to veto. Vote scheduled for Friday April 4. First attempt at UN-authorized military action regarding the blockade.
impact: If passed, authorizes military intervention to reopen Hormuz. Expected Russian/Chinese veto could further isolate the P5 and embolden unilateral action by Gulf states.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Gulf nations back UN Hormuz resolution
The National — GCC urges UNSC action

title: Trump threatens bridges and power plants — 'hasn't even started destroying what's left'
description: Trump posted on Truth Social that the US military 'hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!' Signals escalation to critical civilian infrastructure. Power grid strikes previously postponed to April 6 amid talks. B1 Bridge near Tehran already destroyed killing 8 civilians.
impact: Targeting power plants would affect 90M+ Iranian civilians. Represents massive escalation to civilian infrastructure. April 6 deadline now 3 days away.

sources.name: Al-Monitor — Trump threatens bridges and power plants

title: Dated Brent crude hits $141.36 — highest since 2008; WTI surges 12.9% in single session
description: Dated Brent physical crude spot price surged to $141.36/barrel, highest since the 2008 financial crisis. WTI settled at $113.03 after 12.9% single-session surge — biggest daily gain since conflict began. IEA warned April supply crunch 'much worse than March.' Second strategic reserves release under consideration.
impact: Oil prices approaching levels that triggered the 2008 recession. IEA second reserves release signals unprecedented supply crisis. Stagflation risk accelerating.

title: Hezbollah fires ~130 rockets at northern Israel in massive Passover barrage
description: Hezbollah launched approximately 130 rockets at northern Israel on Passover, with 30+ fired in a single salvo targeting the Kiryat Ata/Haifa area. Four people lightly injured. Largest single Hezbollah barrage this week.
impact: Heaviest Hezbollah rocket barrage this week signals sustained Lebanese front escalation alongside main Iran theater.

title: Kuwait double strike: drones hit Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery; missile hits desalination plant
description: Iranian drones struck Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery (466K bpd) for third time. Separately, missile hit water desalination plant. Kuwait blamed Iran; IRGC denied. Escalates Gulf civilian infrastructure targeting.
impact: Dual strikes on Kuwait energy and water infrastructure cross new humanitarian threshold.

title: Iran claims F-15E shootdown near Qeshm Island — CENTCOM denies
description: IRGC claimed US jet shot down near Qeshm Island in Hormuz. CENTCOM denied, all aircraft safe. Debris analysis shows F-15E wreckage, not F-35 as Iran initially claimed.
impact: If confirmed would be first US aircraft lost in conflict. CENTCOM denial casts doubt but highlights Hormuz airspace risks.

title: Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff and 2 generals during active wartime
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth ousted Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George, Gen. David Hodne, and Maj. Gen. William Green during Week 5 of combat. Unprecedented wartime command shake-up.
impact: Wartime firing of Army's top general signals civil-military tensions. Combined with Hegseth-Rubio ground troops feud raises command stability questions.

sources.name: CBS News — Hegseth ousts Army Chief

title: Trump asserts total war control: 'Israel will do what I tell them'
description: Trump stated Israel will 'do what I tell them' and stop attacking 'when I stop.' Reiterated infrastructure threats. April 6 Hormuz deadline unchanged.
impact: Explicit US command assertion over Israeli operations. No de-escalation before April 6 deadline.

title: US confirms first combat aircraft lost — F-15E shot down over Iran, crew member missing
description: Pentagon confirmed F-15E Strike Eagle from 494th FS (RAF Lakenheath) shot down by Iranian air defenses over central Iran. One crew rescued by US SOF inside Iran. Second missing — Iran offers bounty. First confirmed US fixed-wing combat loss since start of war.
impact: First US combat aircraft loss. Demonstrates Iran's remaining air defense capability despite 35 days of strikes. SAR operations on Iranian soil represent unprecedented US ground presence.

title: US infrastructure campaign escalates — B1 Bridge collapsed, Trump threatens power plants
description: US strikes collapsed B1 Bridge (Tehran-Karaj), tallest in Middle East. 8 killed, 95 wounded. Trump warns 'Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!' marking escalation from military to civilian infrastructure targeting.
impact: Major escalation to infrastructure targeting. Civilian casualties from bridge strike. Trump's power plant threat signals further escalation. 100+ legal scholars call strikes 'possible war crimes.'

title: Iran strikes UAE Habshan gas facility and Bahrain AWS data centers — Gulf infrastructure war expands
description: Iran halted ADNOC's Habshan gas facility (Abu Dhabi's main gas complex) via interceptor debris fire. Also struck Batelco/AWS data centers in Bahrain, disrupting banking/airline systems. UAE engaged 18 ballistic missiles, 4 cruise missiles, 47 drones. 12 injured.
impact: First strike on US tech company infrastructure (AWS). Habshan halt affects UAE gas output. War now targeting digital infrastructure and data centers, expanding attack surface beyond physical facilities.

title: Zarif peace plan signals Tehran internal debate — but Iran rejects ceasefire
description: Former FM Zarif published peace plan in Foreign Affairs: enrichment cap <3.67%, regional facility, reopen Hormuz, nonaggression pact, lift sanctions. Meanwhile Araghchi says 'every bridge will be built back stronger' and rejects ceasefire. Dual signals from Tehran.
impact: First major diplomatic signal from Iranian side. Zarif couldn't publish without tacit approval. But official line remains defiant. UK 40-country Hormuz coalition forming without US.

title: Trump requests record $1.5T defense budget — signals prolonged war posture
description: Trump formally requested $1.5T Pentagon budget ($1.15T base + $350B reconciliation), 44% increase. Includes $50B supplemental for missile/interceptor replacement. Signals US preparing for extended conflict, not de-escalation.
impact: Largest defense budget in US history. Contradicts VP Vance's 'we'll be out of there soon' claim. Goldman Sachs: 30% US recession probability.

title: Second US aircraft lost in 24 hours — A-10 downed during SAR operations
description: An A-10 Warthog was shot down by Iranian air defenses during search-and-rescue operations for the missing F-15E WSO. Pilot rescued. Two Black Hawks also hit. This marks the second US fixed-wing combat loss in a single day — worst since Operation Epic Fury began.
impact: Two aircraft lost in 24h raises questions about SAR doctrine and Iranian AD capabilities. F-15E WSO still missing — political fallout building.

title: Israeli public support for war drops to 45.5% — domestic pressure mounting
description: Haaretz poll shows Israeli support for Iran war dropped from 63% to 45.5%. Combined with $45.8B defense budget strain and no clear exit strategy, Netanyahu faces worst domestic political position since war began.
impact: Falling public support could constrain Netanyahu's military options and accelerate push for ceasefire negotiations.

title: Iranian missile barrage hits 15 sites in central/southern Israel — cluster munitions
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles with cluster munitions at central and southern Israel. 15 impact sites across Tel Aviv metro (Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva, Rosh HaAyin, Givatayim). Fire at Neot Hovav industrial zone in Negev. 1 lightly wounded.
impact: Continued Iranian ability to penetrate Israeli air defenses with ballistic missiles despite 36 days of strikes. Cluster munitions in densely populated areas.

title: Islamic Resistance in Iraq — 19 attacks on US bases in single day
description: Iran-backed Islamic Resistance in Iraq carried out 19 drone and missile attacks on US bases across Iraq in 24 hours, approximately 3x the previous daily attack rate. Targets included PMF HQ in al-Qaim and 34th Brigade HQ in Mosul.
impact: Major escalation on Iraq front. Suggests Iran has directed proxy forces to intensify pressure on US forces in the region.

title: Iran signals Bab el-Mandeb blockade — potential second maritime choke point
description: Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf posted on X hinting at expanding maritime blockade from Hormuz to Bab el-Mandeb Strait via Houthi allies. Would threaten ~12% of global trade through the Red Sea.
impact: If actualized, a dual Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb blockade would be qualitatively different from current crisis — threatening both Persian Gulf and Red Sea shipping simultaneously.

title: US intel: Iran retains ~50% of missile launchers despite 36 days of strikes
description: US intelligence assesses Iran still has roughly half its missile launchers and thousands of one-way attack drones. Iran rapidly repairing bunkers within hours using bulldozers and deploying decoys. Contradicts 'near completion' war narrative.
impact: Calls into question the effectiveness of the air campaign and timeline for ending the war. Iran's resilience exceeds initial US/Israeli estimates.

title: Bushehr Nuclear Plant struck 4th time — 1 killed, IAEA condemns
description: A projectile hit the perimeter of Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, killing one security guard. IAEA Director Grossi expressed 'deep concern.' Iran FM Araghchi warned of 'radioactive fallout on GCC capitals.' Russia strongly condemned the strike.
impact: 4th attack on a nuclear facility crosses an international red line. Risk of radiological disaster. IAEA, Russia, China all condemning. Could trigger further escalation from Iran.

title: Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone struck — 6 plants hit, Iran's 2nd-largest complex
description: US-Israeli strikes hit Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone in Khuzestan Province. Fajr 1 and 2, Rejal, Amir Kabir, Abou Ali, and Bandar-e Imam Khomeini plants struck. 5 injuries. Major escalation against Iranian economic infrastructure.
impact: Destruction of Iran's second-largest petrochemical complex significantly degrades Iran's non-oil export capacity and chemical industry. Combined with 70% steel destruction claim, represents systematic economic warfare.

title: Trump 48-hour ultimatum — 'all Hell' by April 6 if Hormuz not reopened
description: Trump warned Iran has 48 hours (deadline: April 6, 8 PM ET) to reopen Hormuz or face 'all hell.' Iran rejected US ceasefire proposal. 10-day energy strike pause ends April 6. Senator Graham 'completely convinced' of overwhelming force.
impact: Marks the most explicit threat yet against Iranian energy infrastructure. April 6 deadline creates a clear escalation trigger. Iran's rejection of ceasefire signals continued conflict.

title: 5 IRGC Quds Force commanders killed in IDF Beirut hotel strike
description: IDF Navy struck the Ramada Hotel in Raouche, Beirut, killing 5 senior IRGC Quds Force commanders. Represents major escalation in targeting of Iranian command structure outside Iran's borders.
impact: Killing 5 IRGC commanders in a single strike in Beirut decimates Iran's Quds Force leadership in Lebanon. May provoke Iranian retaliation and complicate any ceasefire negotiations.

title: Hezbollah recaptures Khiam and Qantara — 48 IDF soldiers wounded
description: Hezbollah launched ground counterattacks recapturing northern Khiam and re-entering Qantara. 48 IDF soldiers wounded, 40 Hezbollah fighters killed. IDF Division 162 reinforcing. Signals ground war intensifying on Lebanon front.
impact: First significant Hezbollah territorial gains since IDF ground operations began. High IDF casualty rate in single day suggests Hezbollah's ground capabilities remain formidable.

title: Trump issues 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum — April 6 deadline
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social: 'Remember when I gave Iran ten days to MAKE A DEAL or OPEN UP THE HORMUZ STRAIT. Time is running out — 48 hours before all Hell will reign down on them.' Deadline expires Monday April 6, same day energy infrastructure strike pause expires. Indirect negotiations via VP Vance and Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, mediated by Pakistani FM Asim Munir, have stalled.
impact: Major escalation risk. Dual deadline (Hormuz + energy infrastructure pause) creates potential for significant military escalation April 6-7.

title: Iran rejects Trump 48h ultimatum — 'gates of hell will open for you'
description: Iranian military central command described Trump's 48-hour Hormuz threat as 'helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid,' warning 'the gates of hell will open for you.' Ceasefire talks via Pakistan/Egypt/Turkey at standstill. Iran rejected 48-hour ceasefire proposal.
impact: Iran's defiant rejection makes military escalation after April 6 deadline near-certain unless back-channel breakthrough occurs.

sources.name: Middle East Eye — Iran rejects ceasefire

title: Bushehr Nuclear Plant struck for 4th time — IAEA guard killed, Rosatom evacuates
description: Projectile struck auxiliary building near Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, killing one IAEA physical protection staff member. IAEA DG 'deeply concerned.' Russia evacuated 198 more Rosatom staff, ~100 remain. FM Araghchi warned of radioactive fallout to Gulf states.
impact: Nuclear escalation risk. Repeated strikes near operational nuclear plant raise catastrophic fallout concerns.

title: Kuwait Shuwaikh oil complex hit by drone — conflict spreads
description: Drone attack caused fire at Shuwaikh oil sector complex in Kuwait City, housing Kuwait Petroleum Corporation and Ministry of Oil. Fire contained, no injuries. Likely Iran-aligned militia. Represents expansion of proxy targeting to Gulf oil infrastructure.
impact: Kuwait's main oil administration complex targeted for first time. Shows Iran-aligned forces expanding target set.

title: Chinese AI firms tracking US military movements — WaPo investigation
description: Washington Post reports private Chinese tech firms (some with PLA ties) marketing detailed intelligence on US force movements in Iran using AI and open-source data. Tracking carrier groups, aircraft, base activity.
impact: Strategic intelligence concern. China leveraging conflict to develop intel capabilities against US forces.

title: US commando rescue op deep inside Iran — makeshift airbase established
description: US special forces conducted a commando raid inside Iran near Isfahan to rescue the downed F-15E WSO. CIA ran a deception campaign. US established a makeshift airbase inside Iran, then destroyed 2 C-130 transports to prevent capture. Iran reports 5 killed in firefight. First US ground operation deep inside Iran since the 1980 Operation Eagle Claw.
impact: Unprecedented ground operation inside Iran. Demonstrates US willingness to conduct deep-penetration missions. Escalation risk if Iran retaliates for ground incursion.

title: IDF 272 strikes across 14 Iranian provinces — 184 casualties in 24h
description: IDF conducted 272 strikes across 14 Iranian provinces in a single 24-hour period, causing 184 casualties. Targets included Beheshti University medical research institute (near-destroyed) and petrochemical sites. Over 30 Iranian universities targeted since war began.
impact: Highest single-day strike count of the war. Systematic destruction of academic and research infrastructure alongside military targets.

title: Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff during wartime — Rubio feud over ground troops
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth ousted Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other generals during active combat operations. Reports indicate Hegseth and Secretary of State Rubio are 'at each other's throats' over whether to deploy ground troops to Iran. Hegseth supports ground invasion; Rubio is wary.
impact: Military leadership instability during wartime. Internal administration divisions over war strategy visible publicly.

title: Iran expands Gulf state attacks — Kuwait desalination plants, Bahrain BAPCO, UAE 60 projectiles
description: Iran systematically attacked Gulf state civilian and energy infrastructure on April 5: Kuwait desalination plants damaged (90% of drinking water supply), KPC HQ set ablaze, Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery hit 3rd time. Bahrain BAPCO storage tank fireball. UAE engaged 9 ballistic missiles, 1 cruise missile, 50 drones. Represents significant widening beyond Israel/US-Iran axis into civilian infrastructure warfare.
impact: Iran targeting water and power infrastructure across Gulf — 90% of Kuwait's drinking water comes from desalination. Civilian infrastructure warfare expanding.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Kuwait power/water plants hit

title: Trump 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expires April 6 — threatens 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day'
description: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or accept a ceasefire expires Monday April 6 at 8 PM ET. Trump threatened Tuesday will be 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day' in Iran. Iran's military command rejected it as 'helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid' and warned 'gates of hell will open.' Indirect negotiations via Vance-Ghalibaf (Pakistan mediating) at standstill.
impact: Potential major escalation in 24 hours if deadline passes without deal. Trump explicitly threatening civilian energy and transport infrastructure.

sources.name: CBS News — Trump warns Iran 48 hours

title: Iranian missile direct hit on Haifa residential building — civilians trapped under rubble
description: Iranian ballistic missile carrying 400 kg of explosives scored a direct hit on a residential building in Haifa. The missile was NOT intercepted by air defenses. Building partially collapsed with 3-4 people missing under rubble including a child. Warhead may not have fully detonated. Represents first direct hit on occupied residential building in the war.
impact: First unintercepted missile strike on an occupied civilian residential building. Raises questions about air defense gaps and civilian protection.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Haifa missile strike

title: Oman-Iran Hormuz talks — first direct bilateral negotiation on transit passage
description: Oman and Iran held deputy foreign minister-level talks on April 4-5 discussing 'possible options for ensuring smooth passage' through the Strait of Hormuz. Both sides exchanged proposals. 3 Omani ships transited outside Iran's approved corridor. First Western vessel (CMA CGM, France) transited April 3. Signals possible diplomatic path on Hormuz reopening.
impact: First direct bilateral talks on Hormuz transit protocol since blockade began. Could set framework for gradual reopening.

sources.name: The National — Oman and Iran transit measures

title: Iran deploys cluster bomb warheads on ballistic missiles targeting Israeli cities
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles with cluster bomb warheads at Petah Tikva and Haifa on April 6, spreading submunitions across residential areas. 1 woman seriously injured in Petah Tikva, 4 lightly hurt in Haifa including 2 young girls. Continued use of cluster warheads against civilian populations marks escalation in warhead types.
impact: Escalation in weapon types used against civilian areas. Cluster munitions banned by Convention on Cluster Munitions (neither Iran nor Israel are signatories).

title: Hezbollah claims cruise missile strike on Israeli warship — IDF denies
description: Hezbollah claimed it fired a cruise missile at an Israeli warship 68 nautical miles off the Lebanese coast, claiming a direct hit. IDF denied awareness. Unverified videos show a ship ablaze. If confirmed, would represent first successful Hezbollah naval strike at significant range.
impact: If confirmed, major escalation in Hezbollah naval capabilities. Could threaten Israeli naval dominance in eastern Mediterranean.

title: Israel forces closure of Masnaa border crossing (Lebanon-Syria)
description: Israel announced it would strike the Masnaa border crossing between Lebanon and Syria, citing Hezbollah weapons smuggling. The crossing was evacuated and closed on both sides, cutting a key humanitarian and civilian escape route.
impact: Cuts last major land crossing between Lebanon and Syria. Restricts humanitarian access and civilian evacuation routes.

title: Israel kills IRGC Intelligence Chief Khademi in targeted Tehran strike — biggest decapitation since Soleimani
description: Israeli strike at dawn killed Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi, head of IRGC Intelligence Organization (effectively No. 2 in IRGC), along with Quds Force Unit 840 commander Asghar Bagheri. Represents the most significant targeted killing of an Iranian military official since Qasem Soleimani in 2020.
impact: Decapitation of IRGC intelligence apparatus. Expected to trigger significant Iranian retaliation. Removes key figure in Iran's proxy warfare coordination.

sources.name: NBC News — Iran war live updates

title: South Pars petrochemical complex destroyed — 85% of Iran's petrochemical exports offline
description: Israeli strikes obliterated South Pars petrochemical facility at Asaluyeh, Iran's largest petrochemical complex. 5 killed, 170 injured. Combined with previous Mahshahr strikes, 85% of Iran's petrochemical exports now offline per Israeli Defense Ministry. Major economic escalation.
impact: Cripples Iran's non-oil revenue stream. Petrochemicals were Iran's second-largest export sector. Will accelerate economic collapse and civilian hardship.

title: 45-day ceasefire proposal (Islamabad Accord) submitted but Iran rejects temporary Hormuz opening
description: Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey mediators submitted two-phase ceasefire proposal: Phase 1 immediate ceasefire + Hormuz reopening, Phase 2 permanent deal in 15-20 days. Iran declared it will NOT reopen Hormuz as part of temporary ceasefire, demanding full war-damage compensation first.
impact: First formal multi-party ceasefire proposal of the war. Iran's rejection signals potential failure before Trump's Tuesday deadline.

title: Trump threatens to destroy all of Iran in one night — "Power Plant Day and Bridge Day"
description: President Trump held White House press conference stating "The entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night." Threatened to destroy all Iranian power plants and bridges on Tuesday April 7. Set 8pm ET deadline. This represents the most extreme direct threat of total destruction of Iranian civilian infrastructure to date.
impact: Maximum escalation signal. If carried out, would constitute massive targeting of civilian infrastructure. Oil markets bracing for potential spike. Diplomatic window narrowing to hours.

title: Planet Labs indefinite satellite imagery blackout on Iran at US request
description: US government requested Planet Labs impose indefinite blackout on commercial satellite imagery of Iran, retroactive to March 9. Replaces previous 14-day delay with "managed access" model. Independent monitoring of strikes and damage now severely curtailed.
impact: Loss of independent satellite verification for damage claims by both sides. Complicates OSINT analysis and civilian harm documentation.

title: EU announces new sanctions on 19 Iranian regime officials
description: EU High Representative Kallas announced sanctions targeting 19 Iranian officials. Called for Aspides naval mission scale-up. Represents EU escalating economic pressure while remaining non-combatant.
impact: Increases diplomatic isolation of Iran. EU aligning more closely with US position while maintaining separate stance on war.

title: Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure alongside Hormuz — 25% of global energy at risk
description: Senior Iranian adviser Ali Akbar Velayati declared the 'Resistance front views Bab al-Mandeb as it does Hormuz,' threatening to close both straits simultaneously. If executed, ~25% of global energy supply would be blocked. JPMorgan estimates oil could surge to $120-$150/barrel.
impact: Dual-strait closure would be unprecedented. Combines Hormuz (20% of global oil) with Bab al-Mandeb (10% of global trade). Would isolate Europe from Middle Eastern and Asian energy supplies. Houthi cooperation implied.

title: Russia provided Iran satellite intelligence on 50+ Israeli energy targets
description: Ukraine's President Zelensky disclosed that Russia provided Iran with satellite intelligence identifying 50-53 Israeli energy grid targets in three strategic tiers. Orot Rabin power station named as primary target. Russia told Iran that Israel is an 'energy island' where damaging a few components could cause total collapse.
impact: Direct Russian intelligence support for Iranian targeting of Israeli civilian infrastructure. Represents major escalation in Russia-Iran military cooperation. Could trigger Israeli or US response against Russia.

title: Hegseth vows 'most intense day of strikes' — highest volume since Day 1
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Tuesday April 7 will see the most intense day of US strikes on Iran since the war began. Monday was already the highest-volume strike day. Over 15,000 enemy targets hit since Feb 28. Massive escalation as Trump's 8pm ET Hormuz deadline expires tonight.
impact: Unprecedented strike intensity signals possible transition to infrastructure targeting (power plants, bridges) if deadline passes without deal. Iran warns of 'more severe and expansive' response.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Hegseth says strikes increasing

title: All diplomatic channels exhausted — Iran rejects ceasefire, demands permanent end to war
description: Iran formally rejected the 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey mediators. Pakistan mediation track collapsed April 3. Saudi quadrilateral initiative rejected. No active diplomatic channel between Washington and Tehran remains. Iran demands permanent war end with guarantees.
impact: Complete diplomatic breakdown ahead of Trump's infrastructure strike deadline. No intermediary with authority to negotiate on Iran's behalf. Escalation to civilian infrastructure (power plants, bridges) appears imminent.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Iran rejects ceasefire before deadline

title: IRGC Intelligence Chief Khademi and Quds Force commander Bagheri killed in Israeli strike
description: Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi, IRGC intelligence chief and effectively the No. 2 in the IRGC, was killed in an Israeli precision strike along with Quds Force commander Asghar Bagheri. Continues the systematic Israeli campaign to decapitate Iranian military leadership — now 40+ senior officials killed.
impact: Loss of IRGC's top intelligence officer may disrupt Iranian command-and-control. Mojtaba Khamenei responded saying forces are 'not deterred.' IRGC warned of unprecedented retaliation.

sources.name: Fox News — IDF confirms IRGC intel chief killed
Washington Times — Top IRGC intelligence official killed

title: 33+ killed in Tehran residential strikes — Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed
description: Overnight strikes hit residential areas across greater Tehran killing 33+ people. Baharestan county: 13 killed including 6 children under 10. Alborz province: 18 killed including 2 infants. Shahriar: 9 killed. Pardis: 6 bodies recovered. The Rafi-Nia Synagogue in central Tehran was 'completely destroyed.'
impact: High civilian toll including children intensifies international pressure. Synagogue destruction carries additional symbolic weight. Iran calling for human chains around power plants.

sources.name: Middle East Eye — Strikes destroy synagogue

title: Kharg Island hit with new explosions hours before Trump deadline
description: Several explosions reported on Kharg Island — Iran's main oil export terminal handling ~90% of Iran's oil exports. Iranian state media confirmed 'American-Zionist attacks.' Targeting Kharg directly threatens Iran's remaining oil export capability.
impact: Strikes on Iran's primary oil export terminal could cripple Iran's economic lifeline. Combined with Hormuz blockade, Iran's oil revenues near zero.

title: Armed attack on Israeli consulate in Istanbul — 3 gunmen, 1 killed
description: Three assailants with long-barreled weapons opened fire at police outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. 6-minute gunfight: 1 attacker killed, 2 captured, 2 police wounded. Consulate was empty (staff recalled 2023). Attackers from Izmit with ties to religious extremist group.
impact: Conflict spreading to attacks on diplomatic facilities in NATO member Turkey. Could trigger broader security concerns for Israeli embassies worldwide.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Gun clash near Israeli consulate Istanbul

title: US confirmed 50+ military targets struck on Kharg Island hours before deadline
description: US CENTCOM confirmed striking more than 50 military targets on Kharg Island — Iran's main oil export hub handling ~90% of exports. Targets: military bunkers, storage, air defense systems. Officials stated oil infrastructure NOT targeted. Came hours before Trump's 8PM ET deadline.
impact: Second confirmed attack on Kharg Island. 50+ military targets represent massive escalation on Iran's most strategically vital island. Combined with Hormuz blockade, Iran's oil revenues near zero.

sources.name: NBC News — US strikes Kharg Island

title: IDF targets 10 bridges and railways in infrastructure destruction campaign
description: IDF struck 10 bridges and railway sites across Iran: Kashan railway (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan Highway bridge, Qom bridge, Karaj and Qazvin railways. Warned civilians to avoid trains until 21:00. Designed to cripple Iran's ability to move missile launchers.
impact: Systematic destruction of Iranian transport infrastructure. Disrupts military logistics and civilian movement. Train service to Mashhad cancelled.

title: Trump: 'A whole civilization will die tonight' — threatens total infrastructure destruction
description: Trump at White House press conference threatened: 'A whole civilization will die tonight.' Vowed every bridge decimated, every power plant destroyed. Said he is 'not at all' concerned about war crimes. Iran rejected ceasefire with 10 conditions. Deadline 8PM ET.
impact: Most extreme rhetoric from any US president toward another nation. 100+ international law experts warn of war crimes. Guterres warns civilian infrastructure strikes unlawful. Hegseth impeachment push.

title: House Democrats push Hegseth impeachment; Army CoS fired
description: House Democrats announced impeachment articles against Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth over Iran war handling. Hegseth fired Army Chief of Staff Randy George. Retired generals warned Pentagon heading into 'very dangerous' situation.
impact: First impeachment action against a sitting Defense Secretary during active combat operations. Raises questions about civilian-military relations and chain of command.

title: Russia and China veto UN Security Council Hormuz resolution
description: Russia and China vetoed Bahrain-sponsored UNSC resolution to coordinate defensive naval reopening of Strait of Hormuz. Vote 11-2 with 2 abstentions. Blocks multilateral path — leaves Trump's unilateral military threat as only remaining pressure.
impact: UNSC diplomatic path to Hormuz reopening is dead. Only Trump's 8PM ET military deadline and Pakistan mediation remain.

sources.name: Washington Post — Russia China veto Hormuz

title: Iran severs all diplomatic channels with United States
description: Iran officially announced total suspension of all diplomatic and indirect communication channels with the US. Came after Trump's 'whole civilization will die tonight' threat. Pakistan back-channel reportedly still active.
impact: Complete diplomatic breakdown between US and Iran on eve of Trump's infrastructure strike deadline.

title: IRGC strikes Saudi Jubail petrochemical complex with 11 ballistic missiles and 18 drones
description: Iran launched 11 ballistic missiles and 18 drones at Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province in two waves. All missiles intercepted but falling debris struck SABIC petrochemical complex at Jubail Industrial City, igniting massive fire. Partial evacuations ordered.
impact: Escalation to attacking Gulf petrochemical infrastructure. Saudi-Iran tensions at new high. King Fahd Causeway closed.

title: Intel leak: Mojtaba Khamenei unconscious and incapacitated in Qom
description: Leaked US/Israeli intelligence memo claims Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is unconscious and unable to govern from Qom. Wounded in the same Feb 28 strike that killed his father Ali Khamenei. Only AI-generated video shown publicly.
impact: If true, Iran's supreme authority has been incapacitated since war began. Raises fundamental questions about who controls Iran's nuclear and military decisions.

title: Iran fires 6 BM salvos with cluster munitions at Israel on Passover
description: Iran launched 6 separate BM salvos at central/southern Israel on last day of Passover. ~10 missiles per salvo with cluster munitions. ~50 submunition impact sites across Petah Tikva, Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak. 6 wounded including 1 seriously.
impact: Cluster munitions in civilian areas cause long-lasting danger from unexploded submunitions. Targeting on Jewish holiday adds symbolic escalation.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Iran fires salvos
FDD Long War Journal — Cluster munitions

title: IDF destroys Shiraz sonar + petrochemical facilities; 85% capacity inoperable
description: Joint IAF/Navy struck Iran's central sonar production facility and last remaining petrochemical plant producing nitric acid for BM explosives in Shiraz. IDF claims 85%+ of Iran's petrochemical export capacity now inoperable. Also struck large-scale BM site in NW Iran.
impact: Destruction of sonar capabilities degrades submarine fleet. Loss of petrochemical capacity limits future BM production.

sources.name: The Media Line — Petrochemical and missile site

title: 15 Americans wounded in Iranian drone strike on Ali Al Salem Air Base
description: Iranian drone strike hit Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait, wounding 15 American service members. IRGC claimed as Wave 98 of Operation True Promise 4. US total wounded now 380.
impact: Continued Iranian targeting of US bases in Gulf. Draws Kuwait deeper into conflict.

sources.name: Al Mayadeen — Ali Al Salem strike

title: Trump agrees to 2-week bombing suspension — first major de-escalation
description: After 8PM ET deadline passed, Trump agreed to 'suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for two weeks,' conditional on Hormuz reopening. Pakistan PM Sharif mediated. Bloomberg: 'two-week ceasefire to finalize talks.' First ceasefire from US side in 39 days.
impact: First ceasefire offer from US. If Iran accepts and reopens Hormuz, could end hostilities. If rejected, bombing resumes at higher intensity.

title: Iran SNSC formally accepts ceasefire — conditional Hormuz reopening
description: Iran's Supreme National Security Council formally accepted the two-week ceasefire. FM Araghchi confirmed safe passage through Hormuz 'via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.' China gave Khamenei regime a 'last-minute nudge.' Islamabad peace talks confirmed for Friday April 10 with VP Vance leading US delegation. Oil crashed 16-19% in after-hours before rebounding.
impact: First mutual ceasefire in 40 days. Conditional Hormuz reopening could ease oil crisis. If talks succeed, could end conflict. Netanyahu opposed — Israel not formally party to agreement.

sources.name: Axios — US, Iran ceasefire via Pakistan
Al Jazeera — Trump suspends bombing, Iran reopens Hormuz

title: Oil crashes 16-19% on ceasefire — biggest drop since 1991 Gulf War
description: Brent crude plunged ~16% to ~$93/bbl and WTI dropped ~19% to ~$92/bbl in after-hours trading — described as the biggest one-day oil price free fall since the 1991 Gulf War. S&P 500 futures surged 2.4%, Dow futures rose 1,000+ points. By morning, prices rebounded to Brent ~$109, WTI ~$113 as markets expressed skepticism about ceasefire durability.
impact: Massive market volatility. If ceasefire holds and Hormuz reopens, prices could stabilize. If ceasefire fails, prices will spike to new highs.

sources.name: Axios — Oil prices plunge on ceasefire
NBC News — Oil prices plunge, stocks rally

title: Iran violates ceasefire with missile barrage minutes after announcement
description: Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel and Gulf states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain) approximately 10 minutes after Trump's ceasefire announcement. Sirens across Israel. 4 injured Tel Sheva. Jubail petrochemical area struck. Habshan gas fire UAE. Bahrain nationwide sirens. Raises questions about IRGC command compliance.
impact: Ceasefire compliance immediately in question. Gulf states scrambled air defenses. Markets briefly whipsawed before stabilizing on assessment that violation was timing issue, not rejection.

title: Netanyahu excludes Lebanon from ceasefire — IDF operations continue
description: Netanyahu formally stated ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon,' contradicting Pakistan PM Sharif. Israel set 3 conditions for Lebanon talks. IDF operations against Hezbollah continue. This creates a two-track conflict: US-Iran ceasefire while Israel-Hezbollah war persists.
impact: Ceasefire scope dispute. Lebanon front remains active. Hezbollah continued 24 attack waves with 40+ rockets. Potential to undermine broader peace process.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Netanyahu says Lebanon not included
Al-Monitor — Netanyahu backs ceasefire, excludes Lebanon

title: Oil prices crash 16% — biggest drop since 1991 Gulf War — physical oil hits record
description: Brent crashed ~16% to $93, WTI ~19% to $92 after ceasefire. Biggest one-day drop since 1991 Gulf War. However, physical dated Brent hit record $144.42/barrel — highest since 1987. Extreme disconnect between futures (hope) and physical market (scarcity) signals deep structural disruption.
impact: Global financial markets whipsawed. S&P futures +2.4%, Dow +1,000, Nikkei +4.9%. But physical oil scarcity persists. Analysts warn ceasefire may be 'two more weeks of status quo.'

sources.name: NBC News — Oil plunges 15%, stocks rally

title: Lavan Island refinery struck hours after ceasefire announcement
description: Iran's Lavan Island oil refinery attacked at ~06:30 UTC on April 8 — hours after ceasefire announced. Fire broke out but contained. No casualties. Attack occurred post-ceasefire, threatening fragile agreement. Vance warned ceasefire is 'tentative.'
impact: Direct threat to ceasefire durability. Tests whether post-announcement strikes invalidate agreement. Oil markets watching closely.

title: Iran retaliates against UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain post-ceasefire
description: Iran launched retaliatory missile/drone attacks against Gulf states after Lavan strike. Kuwait intercepted 28 drones. Bahrain shot down 31 drones + 6 missiles. 2 Bahraini civilians injured in Sitra. UAE air defenses engaged. Major ceasefire violation expanding conflict to Gulf neighbors.
